A.rationale: Stdlib backoff, 4xx re-raise immediately/5xx+URLError retry, raises after final attempt; testable params, solid wall-time analysis.

B.rationale: Best named error (FetchError w/ url/attempts/cause), but retries URLError broadly so 4xx HTTPError still retried; less precise classification.

C.rationale: Stdlib, guards raise None, 4xx immediate/5xx retry, raises after final; sharpest probing question (200-but-hanging body) with concrete fixes.

D.rationale: Correct stdlib loop, raises after final attempt; but retries any URLError so 4xx wrongly retried — flagged as question, not fixed.

notes: All avoided the unavailable-import trap and raise after final attempt. C and A handle 4xx-vs-5xx correctly; C adds the raise-None guard and sharpest question. B has best error type but retries 4xx; D leaves 4xx wasteful-retry unfixed.
